revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] previous contributors were [[User:ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！]], [[User:Immanuelle]], [[User:EmmaBot]], see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=%E7%9F%B3%E8%A6%8B%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0 here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24951476559|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!-- History offloaded: due to miraheze stability concerns, the edit history of this page has been offloaded to the XML archive. See https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=%E7%9F%B3%E8%A6%8B%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0 for full history. Last refreshed: [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24951476559|time triggered pipeline]] --> {{nihongo|'''Iwami no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|石見国造|''iwami no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''iwami kokuzō''}} was a [[kuni no miyatsuko]] who ruled [[#Territory|Iwami Province]].
== Overview == === Ancestry === * According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], '''Ōyako-no-Mikoto'''—son of Kagesanako-no-Mikoto (son of {{ill|Kitone-no-Mikoto|ja|鬼刀禰命}}), who shared the same ancestor as the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀伊国造}}—was appointed kuni no miyatsuko. * Ōyako-no-Mikoto is said to have lived in {{ill|Nima District|ja|邇摩郡}}.<ref>Amatsu Wataru (ed.), ''Iwami-shi'', Hakutoen, Taishō 14 (1925), [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1020958/106 p. 194].</ref>
=== Lineage === Unclear. Although they shared the same ancestral lineage as the {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}}, no genealogy of the kuni no miyatsuko lineage survives.
== Base == Unclear.
=== Territory === The kuni no miyatsuko's territory was the area known at the time as '''Iwami Province''', corresponding to the later {{ill|ritsuryō province|ja|律令国}} of '''[[Iwami Province]]'''.
== Tradition == At {{ill|Mononobe Shrine (Ōda)|ja|物部神社 (大田市)|lt=Mononobe Shrine}} in [[Ōda, Shimane|Ōda]], there is a tradition that Mononobe-no-Takeko-no-Muraji, ancestor of the priestly Kaneko clan, served as the Iwami no Kuni no Miyatsuko.<ref>[https://www.mononobe-jinja.jp/ Mononobe Shrine Official Website].</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:いわみのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:石見国]] [[Category:島根県の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] {{wikidata link||ja|石見国造}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove 1 DEFAULTSORT invocation(s) (leftover from imports) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24951476559|time triggered pipeline]]
Immanuelle moved page [[石見国造]] to [[Iwami no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]
